rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use assign statements only for implementation-level output bindings with existing features or declared variables.
Generate boolean range expressions using declared helper variables with matching types.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid within annex blocks and reference only visible identifiers.
Component type contracts should use feature names and constants; implementation contracts should bind to subcomponents.
Ensure type consistency between AGREE expressions and AADL data types to avoid validation errors.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`
Assumptions and guarantees must be placed in valid component scopes
Boolean range checks should use helper variables with explicit type declarations
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type scopes
Do not reference AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions
Do not use opaque Boolean data in numeric range comparisons
irrelevant_topics: Model fusion and owner scope rules
